name: Winter Olympics
description: The Winter Games — hosts, sports, and snowy legends

questions.q: Which French town hosted the first Winter Olympic Games in 1924?

source.notes: Originally called 'International Winter Sports Week.' Retroactively awarded Olympic status.

questions.q: Which country hosted the 2014 Winter Olympics in Sochi?

source.notes: Most expensive Olympics ever: $50+ billion.

questions.q: In what year did the Winter Olympics move to being held on a different 4-year cycle than the Summer Olympics?

source.notes: 1994 Lillehammer Games were only 2 years after 1992 Albertville. After this, staggered on 2-year cycle.

questions.q: Which Canadian city hosted the 1988 Winter Olympics?

source.notes: Featured Jamaican bobsled team (inspired 'Cool Runnings') and Eddie 'the Eagle' Edwards.

questions.q: Which Asian city hosted the Winter Olympics in 1998?

source.notes: Nagano, Japan. Japan also hosted 1972 (Sapporo). Third time in Asia.

questions.q: How many Winter Olympic sports are typically contested today?

source.notes: 15 sports. Includes skiing, skating, hockey, bobsled, luge, biathlon, curling, etc.

questions.q: Which Winter Olympic sport was introduced at the 1998 Nagano Games, becoming wildly popular?

source.notes: Introduced as 'snowboarding,' bringing X Games-style action to the Olympics.

questions.q: Who was the first African American woman to win an individual Winter Olympic gold medal?

source.notes: Won speed skating gold at Beijing 2022 (500m). First Black woman to win Olympic speedskating individual gold.

questions.q: How often were the Winter Olympics held from 1994 onwards?

source.notes: Every 4 years, alternating with Summer Olympics (on a 2-year offset).

questions.q: Which Norwegian biathlete won 13 Winter Olympic medals across the 1998–2014 Games?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: original Q wrongly claimed Bjørndalen held the all-time Winter Olympics medal record — fellow Norwegian Marit Bjørgen has 15 (cross-country skiing). Bjørndalen's 13 medals (8G/4S/1B) remain the men's record.

questions.q: The 1980 'Miracle on Ice' saw which US team upset the Soviet Union?

source.notes: US college/amateur players beat the dominant Soviet professionals at Lake Placid. US went on to win gold.

questions.q: Salt Lake City hosted the Winter Olympics in what year?

source.notes: Marred by pre-Games bribery scandal during the selection process. First major Olympics after 9/11.

questions.q: Which Austrian city has hosted the Winter Olympics twice (1964 and 1976)?

source.notes: Second hosting in 1976 came after Denver, CO withdrew over environmental concerns.

questions.q: Which city hosted the 2022 Winter Olympics?

source.notes: First city to host both Summer (2008) and Winter Olympics. Some events held under strict COVID protocols.

questions.q: In which sport do teams slide stones on ice, sweeping with brooms?

source.notes: Scottish origins. Became full medal sport in 1998. 'Chess on ice.'

questions.q: The Winter Olympic sport of biathlon combines skiing and what?

source.notes: Cross-country skiing + rifle shooting. Originated from Norwegian military training.

questions.q: Which Winter Olympic sport involves sliding head-first down a track on a small sled?

source.notes: Luge is feet-first on back. Skeleton is head-first on stomach. Both can exceed 80 mph.

questions.q: Which country has won the most Winter Olympic medals all-time?

source.notes: Norway: ~405 medals. USA: ~330. Dominant in skiing and biathlon.

questions.q: How many figure skating disciplines are contested at the Winter Olympics?

source.notes: Men's, women's, pairs, ice dance, team event. Added team in 2014.

questions.q: Which US city will host the 2034 Winter Olympics?

source.notes: Selected in 2024 by IOC. Salt Lake City's second Winter Olympics (first was 2002).
